methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $400M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Type One Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Marvel Fusion is a Series B-stage nuclear energy company that has raised $400M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparative universe of 11 peers spanning the nuclear sector: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Type One Energy, TerraPower, Kairos Power, X-Energy, and Oklo.

A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within Marvel Fusion's narrow tier; while 8 nuclear peers exist broadly, fewer than 3 met the stage and valuation criteria needed for reliable triangulation. The stage-progression method proved applicable, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $400M raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $760M.

The triangulated valuation range is $646M (low) to $874M (high), with a median of $760M. This single-method result carries material uncertainty given reliance on a stage-based multiplier rather than direct peer benchmarking. Key sensitivities include the appropriateness of the 1.9x multiplier for frontier nuclear technology at this stage, Marvel Fusion's relative technology and team positioning within the peer set, and near-term funding environment. A more robust conclusion would require tightening the nuclear peer set through additional disclosures or access to recent transaction data in the sector.
